analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet claims some U.S. influenza deaths were actually COVID-19 cases per CDC’s Robert Redfield, highlights large U.S. flu figures, and demands transparency from the CDC about how many deaths were COVID-related. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) — The tweet criticizes U.S. public health reporting/competence and implies misclassification or lack of transparency, casting the U.S. (a Western country) in a negative light. This aligns with portraying the West as unreliable or deceptive, thus partially fitting CH1. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) — No mention of China’s benevolence or international role; not promoted. CH3 (China has an epic history) — Not referenced. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) — No comparison to Chinese governance/values; not present. CH5 (The government of the Chinese Communist Party succeeds) — No claims about CCP performance; not present. CH6 (China is interesting culturally/naturally) — Not relevant. Conclusion: Only CH1 applies, via implicit criticism of the U.S./West.
